free_text: Nuclear structure studies and particle-rotor model
15 MeV tritons from the McMaster Tandem
The targets were prepared from samples of isotopically
enriched Sm2O3, purchased from the Isotope Sales
Division of the Oak Ridge National Laboratory. The
oxides were reduced by heating with lanthanum metal in
vacuum, and the samarium metal was simultaneously
vacuum-evaporated onto carbon foils. For the 149Sm
target the carbon foil had a thickness of about
20 microg/cm2, but for greater safety the radioactive
151Sm  was collected on a thicker foil of about
sample, as stated by the supplier, was 97.72% 149Sm,
time the (t,p) measurements were made some of the
original 151Sm would have decayed to 151Eu, leaving an
estimated 86.5% abundance of 151Sm in the target.
From intensities of elastically scattered beam particle
it was estimated the target thicknesses were about
200 microg/cm2 and about 50 microg/cm2 for 149Sm and
